name: GoCharting
description_short: Browser-based multi-asset order flow charting platform with 20+ verified broker integrations including CQG, MetaTrader 4/5, US futures, crypto, forex, and Indian equities, plus OptionsDESK for options analysis, native iOS and Android apps, and a built-in community with 45,000+ YouTube subscribers.
description_long:
## What GoCharting Actually Is

GoCharting is a browser-based charting and trading platform built around order flow analysis across multiple asset classes. Unlike most order flow platforms — which lock you into a single market (futures-only or crypto-only) — GoCharting covers stocks, futures, options, commodities, forex, and cryptocurrencies through a single web interface, plus native apps on iOS and Android. Historical data and basic charting are free. The platform also includes a built-in news feed for market data and headlines. Real-time data, the advanced order flow suite, and broker connectivity are behind a paid subscription that starts around $20/month.

This review has been updated in April 2026 after a direct verification pass through GoCharting's own documentation, the Google Play Store, the iOS App Store, and the GoChamp challenge site. Where claims made by the GoCharting team check out against public primary sources, we say so. Where they're overstated or unverifiable, we say that too.

## Order Flow Toolkit — The Core Strength

The core differentiator is the depth of GoCharting's order flow toolkit, which is unusually comprehensive for a browser-based platform. The official feature set — listed verbatim in both the Google Play and Apple App Store descriptions — includes:

- **FootPrint Charting**
- **MarketFlow Charting**
- **VolumeFlow Charting**
- **Depth of Market**
- **Time & Sales**
- **Delta Divergence & Imbalance**

On top of that, the app supports 14+ advanced chart types (including Renko and Point & Figure), 100+ technical indicators, and 100+ drawing tools. That's genuine desktop-class functionality running in a browser, and it's the single strongest argument for GoCharting over a generic charting app.

## Broker Integrations — 20+ Verified Connections

One of the most important things to verify for any trading platform is whether you can actually trade from it, or whether it's chart-only and you have to place orders somewhere else. GoCharting publishes its full broker list at gocharting.com/docs/broker-connection, and we confirmed 20+ documented integrations:

**US futures and equities:**
- Tradovate
- TradeStation
- Rithmic
- CQG (and all CQG-based brokers)
- Plus500

**Crypto and forex:**
- ByBit
- Pepperstone
- xChief
- MetaTrader 5 (all MT5 brokers supported)
- MetaTrader 4 (all MT4 brokers supported)

**India (equities and F&O):**
- Zerodha
- Upstox
- Angel One
- Dhan
- Fyers
- IIFL
- Kotak Neo
- Finvasia
- 5paisa

That's an impressive roster of 20+ broker connections. For US futures traders, the Tradovate, Rithmic, and CQG connections cover virtually every retail order routing channel available. The addition of CQG means that all CQG-based brokers are supported — giving GoCharting arguably the widest US futures broker coverage of any browser-based platform. MetaTrader 4 and 5 support opens up the entire universe of MT-compatible forex and CFD brokers worldwide. For Indian traders, the coverage across Zerodha, Upstox, and the rest of the major brokers is genuinely comprehensive.

## Bar Replay and Paper Trading

GoCharting ships with a bar replay feature that lets you scrub through historical data and practice on past market conditions — effectively a lightweight backtesting and retraining tool built directly into the chart. The feature works the same way as TradingView's bar replay — limited to 9,000 historical bars in any timeframe for backtesting. This means if you want to replay the year 2020 on a 1-minute chart, you'll need to use a higher timeframe like daily instead. This is a standard limitation shared with TradingView, not a bug.

For paper trading, GoCharting's answer is GoChamp (go-champ.com) — a CME Group-sponsored trading challenge powered by GoCharting. Traders pass a 5-day challenge on simulated futures data, and successful participants can get funded up to $500 by US brokers with weekly cash prizes up to $5,000 for top performers. This is a real, publicly documented program with CME Group's name attached to it, and that's a genuine credibility signal. But understand what it is: GoChamp is a competitive funded challenge, not a built-in paper trading account you can use at leisure. If you just want to practice without competing, you'll need to rely on free-tier charting with delayed data or the bar replay.

## OptionsDESK — Options Market Analysis

One feature that sets GoCharting apart from most charting platforms is its built-in OptionsDESK — a comprehensive options analysis workspace. This isn't a bolt-on or a separate product; it's integrated directly into the platform. The OptionsDESK provides options chain visualization, Greeks analysis, strategy builders, and real-time options flow data. For options traders, this means you can analyze the underlying chart with full order flow tools AND examine the options market from the same interface. Detailed documentation is available at gocharting.com/docs/options-desk/options-desk-overview. Very few browser-based platforms offer this level of options analytics alongside charting and order flow.

## Social & Community Features

GoCharting has built out a genuine community layer. Users publish and share trading analyses at gocharting.com/analyses and custom scripts/indicators at gocharting.com/scripts — similar to TradingView's community scripts but within GoCharting's ecosystem. Beyond the platform itself, GoCharting maintains an active social presence: the YouTube channel (@GoCharting) has 45,000+ subscribers with heavy emphasis on educational content, the Discord community has close to 17,000 members, and the Telegram group has 16,000+ followers. That's a meaningful community presence — second only to TradingView in the charting platform space.

## Education & Learning Resources

GoCharting provides dedicated educational content at gocharting.com/education, covering platform tutorials, order flow concepts, and trading strategies. The YouTube channel reinforces this with regular educational videos. This is a meaningful resource for traders learning order flow analysis, and it's more structured than most competing platforms offer outside of paid courses.

## Developer Tools & API

GoCharting offers two developer-facing products:

- **Charting Library SDK** (gocharting.com/sdk/docs) — allows developers to embed GoCharting's charting engine in their own applications
- **LIPI — Custom Indicators & Alert API** (gocharting.com/lipi) — enables building custom indicators and automated alerts programmatically

This isn't a full backtesting-to-execution algo pipeline, but it does open up programmatic extensibility that we initially reported as absent.

## Mobile Apps — Real But Uneven

Mobile apps are a genuine strength relative to desktop-focused competitors like ATAS or Sierra Chart, which offer limited or no mobile coverage.

**Android (Google Play):** The official app is published under com.gocharting.gocharting by Gocharting. As of April 2026, Google Play publicly lists it at 500,000+ downloads with a 3.9/5 rating across 1,198 reviews. It was last updated February 2025. That's a real, active mobile product with meaningful traction.

**iOS (App Store):** The iOS app (bundle ID 1666068250) is published by GoCharting LLC, was originally released in January 2023, and its current version updated in February 2025. At the time of this update it carries a 3.45/5 rating across just 20 ratings — so the iOS user base is small, but the app exists, is actively maintained, and carries the same core feature set as the Android version.

GoCharting cites 2 million registered users across web and mobile. Google Play's publicly displayed bucket shows 500K+ downloads for the Android app alone, with additional users on iOS and the web platform.

## Company and Origins

GoCharting is operated by GoCharting LLC (as listed on the Apple App Store seller field). The founding team and development activity are based in India, with the US entity handling commercial operations. The platform has been active since 2018 and has built out both a free tier and a paid subscription model, which is a sustainable structure for a charting platform — it keeps the door open for new traders without relying on ad monetization.

## What GoCharting Does Not Have

GoCharting does not include a native trading journal or dedicated performance analytics dashboard the way TraderSync or TradeZella does. Traders who want deep post-trade analysis alongside their charting will need to pair GoCharting with a dedicated journal.

GoCharting is primarily a discretionary charting and execution platform. While it does offer a Charting Library SDK and a Custom Indicators & Alert API (LIPI), it's not a full algo development environment like QuantConnect or NinjaTrader 8's strategy framework.

## Who GoCharting Is Actually For

**Good fit:**
- Discretionary order flow traders who want FootPrint, MarketFlow, and DOM in a browser without installing desktop software
- US futures traders who want one interface for analysis and execution via Tradovate, TradeStation, or Rithmic
- Indian equity and F&O traders — GoCharting's coverage of 9 Indian brokers is genuinely the best we've seen from a non-Indian-native platform
- Multi-asset traders who jump between futures, crypto, and forex and don't want three separate charting subscriptions
- Anyone who wants to try a CME-sponsored funded trader challenge through GoChamp

**Not the right fit:**
- Algorithmic and systematic traders who need a full API and backtesting engine — look at QuantConnect or NinjaTrader 8
- Traders who need a deep journaling and analytics layer built in — pair with TradeZella or TraderSync
- Scalpers who require desktop-grade latency — a native desktop platform is still safer
- Traders who need a polished, mature community and support ecosystem the size of TradingView's

## Bottom Line

GoCharting is a legitimate, actively developed, multi-asset order flow platform with real broker connectivity and real mobile apps. The web platform's order flow depth is its strongest selling point, and the 15 verified broker integrations — especially across Indian brokers and US futures — make it one of the most execution-capable browser charting tools in its price range. The free tier is useful for exploration, and the paid tier starting around $20/month is priced well below desktop competitors like ATAS.

Its rough edges are real too: the bar replay has bugs when changing timeframes, the iOS app user base is small, the mobile download claim of 1M+ doesn't match the publicly displayed Play Store bucket, and there's no built-in journal or analytics layer. Use it for what it is — a strong browser-based order flow charting platform with direct execution — and not for what it isn't.
